name: Economic Growth Corporation
description_short: Mission-driven nonprofit providing HUD-approved housing counseling, community development, and homebuyer assistance since 1982 across Illinois and beyond.
description_long:
Economic Growth Corporation (GROWTH) is a national nonprofit organization founded in 1982 with a mission to strengthen underserved communities through housing development, economic development, and strategic partnerships. Based on their website, they operate as a comprehensive community development organization rather than a consumer finance service in the traditional sense. Their work focuses on revitalization of neighborhoods and creating long-term economic opportunity for low-income communities.

GROWTH offers several interconnected services: HUD-approved housing counseling delivered by HUD-Certified Housing Counselors, homebuyer education programs (including bilingual offerings), foreclosure prevention counseling, financial literacy training, and a homebuyer assistance program (Live-Work Rock Island) that has helped 482 employer-assisted homebuyers. They also operate three subsidiaries—Growth General Contracting, Home Base Property Management, and Central States Development Partners—that handle construction, property management, and deployment of New Market Tax Credits respectively. Beyond counseling, they develop affordable housing (402 multifamily units, 41 single-family homes) and commercial spaces.

What distinguishes GROWTH is their rigorous HUD certification requiring ongoing reviews and their network of HUD-Certified counselors who pass national exams. They've served 3,500+ Illinois households, assisted 900 individuals in becoming homeowners, and provided foreclosure prevention counseling to 1,940+ individuals. Their integrated approach combines counseling with direct housing development and property management, creating a comprehensive ecosystem rather than standalone advisory services. They also deploy significant capital ($321M+ in New Market Tax Credits) to support business development in distressed communities.

For consumers, GROWTH represents a legitimate nonprofit resource for housing counseling and homebuyer education, particularly those in Illinois or seeking employer-assisted homebuying programs. The primary caveat is that their services are geographically concentrated and development-focused; they are not a credit repair, debt relief, or personal finance company. Their value proposition centers on housing stability and homeownership pathways rather than credit score improvement or emergency lending.
pros: HUD-approved housing counseling agency since 2003 with rigorous certification standards and HUD-Certified counselors
Served 3,500+ households with demonstrated track record of assisting 900 homeowners in Illinois
Bilingual financial literacy and homebuyer education offerings (English and Spanish)
Integrated approach combining free counseling with actual affordable housing development and employer-assisted programs
Foreclosure prevention counseling specifically available (1,940+ individuals assisted)
Live-Work Rock Island program resulted in 482 employer-assisted homebuyers with down payment support
Central States Development Partners deploys $321M+ in New Market Tax Credits to underserved communities
cons: Services and housing programs appear to be primarily Illinois-focused despite 'national' nonprofit designation
Not a credit repair or credit improvement service—cannot dispute credit report errors or improve credit scores
No emergency lending, personal loans, or immediate financial assistance offered
Homebuyer programs may have geographic or income eligibility restrictions not detailed on website
Counseling and education services do not provide debt settlement, consolidation, or debt relief solutions
best_for: Illinois residents seeking free HUD-approved housing counseling and homebuyer education
Prospective homebuyers interested in employer-assisted down payment programs (Rock Island area)
Homeowners facing foreclosure seeking prevention counseling and stabilization resources
Underserved community members seeking financial literacy and housing stability guidance
diagnosis: GROWTH is best for Illinois residents and underserved community members seeking free, high-quality housing counseling, homebuyer education, and pathways to homeownership—particularly those with employer-assisted programs available. The main caveat is that this is a housing-focused nonprofit, not a credit repair, lending, or debt management service; consumers seeking credit score improvement, emergency loans, or debt relief should look elsewhere.
services: HUD-approved housing counseling from HUD-Certified Housing Counselors
Financial literacy training (offered in English and Spanish)
Down payment assistance programs (Moline Down Payment Housing Program)
Affordable multifamily rental housing development and management
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
